task_description: Create a new Java project named test3 and run its main program.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered on the desktop shortcut for IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.3. The icon is a black square with the white letters "IJ" over overlapping orange, red, and blue geometric shapes, and the label below reads "IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.3." In the full-screen desktop view, this shortcut is located among other desktop icons on the left side of a Windows 11 desktop.
caption.think: The user intends to open IntelliJ IDEA by double-clicking its desktop shortcut.
caption.action: Double-click the IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.3 desktop shortcut.
caption.expectation: IntelliJ IDEA should launch, and its application window or loading splash screen should appear.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered on the rounded rectangular button labeled “新建项目” in the IntelliJ IDEA welcome window. This is a top-area UI control, not a canvas object or blank region. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop, showing the welcome screen with a project list in the center and buttons including “新建项目”, “打开”, and “克隆仓库” near the top right.
caption.think: The user wants to start creating a new IntelliJ IDEA project from the welcome screen.
caption.expectation: A New Project dialog or project-creation screen should open so the user can configure a new project.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered on the item labeled “Java” in the language list under the “新建项目” panel. This is a list-style UI control inside the new-project dialog, shown with a coffee-cup icon to the left of the word “Java.” Nearby options include Kotlin, Groovy, Python, and “空项目,” which is currently highlighted in blue. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open to the New Project window on top of the welcome screen, with fields on the right for project name and location and a “创建” button at the bottom right.
caption.think: The user wants to switch the project type from the currently selected empty project to a Java project so the Java-specific project setup options appear.
caption.action: Click the “Java” item in the project type list.
caption.expectation: The “Java” project type should become selected, and the right side of the New Project dialog should update to show Java project configuration options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered on the text field containing the project name “untitled.” This is a UI control in the New Project dialog, specifically the editable name input box outlined in blue, indicating it is active or selected. Below it are project location details and a “创建 Git 仓库” checkbox. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” window is open on the desktop with “Java” selected in the project-type list on the left and the project settings panel on the right.
caption.think: The user is clicking into the project name input field, likely to place focus there so the default name can be edited or replaced.
caption.action: Click the project name text field containing “untitled.”
caption.expectation: The name field should remain focused with a text cursor ready for editing, allowing the project name to be changed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, a semitransparent red drag path runs horizontally across the project name input box that currently contains the text “untitled.” The path starts near the right side of the text and extends leftward across the word, indicating a text-selection drag within the active blue-outlined name field rather than moving a panel. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” dialog is open with “Java” selected in the project type list, and the project name field at the top right is focused.
caption.think: The user is dragging across the existing project name to highlight the default text so it can be replaced with a new project name.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the project name text inside the “untitled” field, drag left across the word to select the entire name, then release.
caption.expectation: The text “untitled” should become highlighted, ready to be replaced by typing a new project name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered inside the blue-outlined project name input box, which currently contains the full text “untitled.” This is a UI control in the top-right portion of the IntelliJ IDEA “新建项目” dialog, not a blank region or canvas object. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA’s New Project window is open over the desktop with “Java” selected in the project type list, the location field below, and the name field already active-looking with a blue border.
caption.think: The user is clicking the project name text field again to place the caret or confirm focus there before editing the default project name.
caption.action: Click the project name input box containing “untitled.”
caption.expectation: The name field should become or remain focused, with the text cursor placed in the field so the project name can be edited.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, a semitransparent red drag path runs horizontally across the blue-outlined project name input box containing the text “untitled.” The path spans most of the field from the left side toward the right side, indicating a text-selection drag across the existing project name rather than moving a window or panel. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” dialog is open with “Java” selected in the project type list, and the name field at the upper right is active.
caption.think: The user is dragging across the current project name to select the existing text so it can be replaced with a new name.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the text inside the project name field containing “untitled,” drag along the shown horizontal path across the word toward the right side of the field, then release.
caption.expectation: The text in the project name field should become highlighted, ready to be overwritten by typing a new project name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the IntelliJ IDEA 新建项目 dialog focused on the 名称 input box. The text field has a red validation outline and currently contains the partial lowercase text “te”. Above it is a red warning bubble stating that only allowed characters may be used. A Chinese input candidate bar is open directly under the field, offering candidates based on the typed letters. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop with Java selected as the project type, and this name field is the active text entry area for the new project.
caption.think: The user is entering a new project name and is replacing the previously selected default name with the intended name TEST. Since typing takes precedence, this step is to continue text entry in the active project name field.
caption.action: Type “TEST” into the project name field, replacing the selected default content.
caption.expectation: The project name field should update to show the typed project name TEST, and the location/path preview below should refresh to use that new name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the IntelliJ IDEA project name field with the text “test” entered. A Chinese input candidate bar is open directly beneath the field, showing numbered candidates including “1 test,” “2 特殊,” “3 特色,” “4 特俗,” and others. Above the field, a brown warning tooltip states that the directory for “test” is not empty. The full-screen view shows IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” window with Java selected, the name field active, and the desktop visible behind it.
caption.think: The user has finished typing the project name and now wants to confirm the current input/composition so the candidate bar closes and the entered name remains in the field.
caption.expectation: The current text input should be confirmed, the candidate suggestion bar should disappear, and the project name field should remain set to “test,” with the warning about the non-empty directory still visible.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in area shows IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” dialog with the “名称” input box active. The field currently contains the text “test,” and a brown warning tooltip above it says that the directory for test is not empty. No candidate list is visible now, indicating the text entry is being directly edited. The full-screen view shows IntelliJ IDEA on the Windows desktop with Java selected as the project type, the location field below set to a D-drive path, and the Create button at the bottom right.
caption.think: The user is continuing to edit the project name in the active name field by appending a character, likely to change “test” into “test3” and avoid the existing-directory conflict.
caption.action: Type “3” into the active project name field, appending it to the existing text “test.”
caption.expectation: The project name should update to “test3,” and the project path preview below should refresh accordingly; the non-empty directory warning may disappear if that new folder name does not already exist.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a blue rounded rectangular button labeled “创建(C)” with a semitransparent red X centered directly on it. To its right is a dark button labeled “取消.” This is a UI control in the dialog’s bottom-right action area. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA’s “新建项目” window is open on the Windows desktop. Java is selected as the project type, the project name field shows “test3,” and the dialog is ready to proceed with creation.
caption.think: The user has finished configuring the new project and wants to confirm the dialog by pressing the Create button so IntelliJ IDEA starts creating the Java project.
caption.expectation: The button should activate, the new project dialog should close, and IntelliJ IDEA should begin creating and opening the new project.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X placed directly over the dark code editing area in IntelliJ IDEA, centered on the line containing the string literal "Hello and welcome!" near the end of the statement `System.out.printf("Hello and welcome!");`. This is not on a toolbar button or side panel control; it is on the code text/canvas region of the editor. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the project `test3`, `Main.java` is the active tab, the project tree is visible on the left, and the editor contains a simple Java `main` method with the current line highlighted in the code area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place the text cursor inside the Java editor on that line so they can edit or continue working with the `Hello and welcome!` print statement.
caption.action: Click the code editor text area on the line with `System.out.printf("Hello and welcome!");` where the red X is centered.
caption.expectation: The editor should gain focus at that spot, placing the caret there or selecting that code position, with no dialog opening; this is a canvas/object selection in the code editor entering text-edit focus.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the small three-horizontal-lines button, the main menu/hamburger icon, in the top-left corner of the IntelliJ IDEA window. It sits in the purple title/toolbar area, immediately to the right of the IntelliJ IDEA logo and to the left of the project label “test3.” This is a toolbar/menu control, not part of the code editor canvas. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the `test3` project and `Main.java` visible; the project tree is on the left and the Java editor is on the right.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open IntelliJ IDEA’s main application menu or navigation panel from the top-left toolbar to access additional commands.
caption.action: Click the top-left hamburger menu button with three horizontal lines.
caption.expectation: The menu button should become active and open IntelliJ IDEA’s main menu or a navigation/menu panel from the top-left area.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the first item in a dark dropdown menu: the menu entry with a green play triangle icon and the label “运行 'Main.java'(U)”. This is a UI control in a popup menu opened from IntelliJ IDEA’s top toolbar area, not the code canvas. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the `test3` project and `Main.java`; the Run menu is expanded near the top-left of the editor window, and the highlighted top command is the run action for the current file.
caption.think: The user likely wants to execute the current Java file directly from the Run menu.
caption.action: Click the menu item “运行 'Main.java'(U)” with the green play icon.
caption.expectation: That run command should be selected, the dropdown menu should close, and IntelliJ IDEA should start running `Main.java`, typically opening or updating the Run tool window with program output.
